name: Broad 100 personal values
description: An original, editable 100-value vocabulary spanning agency, relationships, ethics, growth, purpose, wellbeing, exploration, work, society, and stability.
citation: Values Tool original broad values catalog (2026), informed by common non-proprietary values vocabularies.
licenseNote: Original catalog released with the application under the MIT License; not a reproduction of a proprietary card sort.

values.name: Autonomy
values.definition: Directing my own choices and life

values.name: Freedom
values.definition: Having meaningful room to choose and act

values.name: Independence
values.definition: Relying on my own judgment and capacity

values.name: Courage
values.definition: Acting despite fear or meaningful risk

values.name: Initiative
values.definition: Starting useful action without waiting

values.name: Self-respect
values.definition: Treating my own dignity as non-negotiable

values.name: Authenticity
values.definition: Living consistently with what I genuinely believe

values.name: Discipline
values.definition: Following through despite distraction or discomfort

values.name: Resilience
values.definition: Recovering and adapting after difficulty

values.name: Self-knowledge
values.definition: Understanding my motives, patterns, and limits

values.name: Belonging
values.definition: Feeling accepted within a group or place

values.name: Family
values.definition: Sustaining loving and dependable family bonds

values.name: Friendship
values.definition: Building close and mutually supportive friendships

values.name: Intimacy
values.definition: Sharing inner experience with trusted people

values.name: Love
values.definition: Giving and receiving deep care

values.name: Loyalty
values.definition: Remaining faithful to people and commitments

values.name: Trust
values.definition: Being reliable and able to rely on others

values.name: Compassion
values.definition: Responding to suffering with concern and action

values.name: Empathy
values.definition: Understanding another person's experience

values.name: Community
values.definition: Participating in reciprocal life with others

values.name: Honesty
values.definition: Representing reality and intentions truthfully

values.name: Integrity
values.definition: Keeping actions consistent with stated principles

values.name: Justice
values.definition: Supporting fair treatment and institutions

values.name: Fairness
values.definition: Applying standards without improper favoritism

values.name: Responsibility
values.definition: Owning commitments, choices, and consequences

values.name: Accountability
values.definition: Answering candidly for results and repairing harm

values.name: Respect
values.definition: Recognizing the dignity and boundaries of others

values.name: Humility
values.definition: Holding an accurate, uninflated view of oneself

values.name: Forgiveness
values.definition: Releasing resentment while retaining discernment

values.name: Virtue
values.definition: Cultivating morally excellent character

values.name: Curiosity
values.definition: Seeking new understanding and experience

values.name: Learning
values.definition: Increasing knowledge through study and reflection

values.name: Mastery
values.definition: Developing excellent skill through sustained practice

values.name: Wisdom
values.definition: Using sound judgment informed by experience

values.name: Creativity
values.definition: Producing original and useful ideas or forms

values.name: Open-mindedness
values.definition: Revising beliefs when evidence warrants

values.name: Challenge
values.definition: Choosing demanding work that develops capacity

values.name: Excellence
values.definition: Pursuing unusually high standards

values.name: Achievement
values.definition: Completing work that matters

values.name: Growth
values.definition: Continuing to develop capacity and maturity

values.name: Meaning
values.definition: Connecting life to purposes experienced as significant

values.name: Purpose
values.definition: Maintaining direction around what matters most

values.name: Contribution
values.definition: Leaving people or systems better through my effort

values.name: Service
values.definition: Using my abilities for others or a larger cause

values.name: Stewardship
values.definition: Caring for shared resources and future needs

values.name: Legacy
values.definition: Creating benefits that endure beyond me

values.name: Faith
values.definition: Orienting life through religious or spiritual conviction

values.name: Spirituality
values.definition: Developing connection to the sacred or transcendent

values.name: Devotion
values.definition: Giving sustained commitment to a person, practice, or cause

values.name: Hope
values.definition: Sustaining possibility and constructive expectation

values.name: Health
values.definition: Supporting physical and mental functioning

values.name: Vitality
values.definition: Living with energy and aliveness

values.name: Peace
values.definition: Reducing destructive conflict within and around me

values.name: Balance
values.definition: Allocating attention without chronic distortion

values.name: Rest
values.definition: Protecting time for recovery and renewal

values.name: Pleasure
values.definition: Making room for enjoyable experience

values.name: Joy
values.definition: Welcoming delight and deep gladness

values.name: Play
values.definition: Engaging in freely chosen, absorbing activity

values.name: Comfort
values.definition: Maintaining reasonable ease and relief from distress

values.name: Safety
values.definition: Protecting people from avoidable harm

values.name: Adventure
values.definition: Seeking novel and stretching experience

values.name: Wonder
values.definition: Remaining receptive to awe and mystery

values.name: Discovery
values.definition: Finding what was previously unknown

values.name: Variety
values.definition: Including meaningful change and difference

values.name: Spontaneity
values.definition: Leaving room for unplanned action

values.name: Risk
values.definition: Accepting uncertainty for a worthwhile possibility

values.name: Experimentation
values.definition: Learning by trying and observing

values.name: Imagination
values.definition: Forming possibilities beyond present conditions

values.name: Beauty
values.definition: Appreciating compelling form, harmony, and expression

values.name: Nature
values.definition: Maintaining attentive connection with the living world

values.name: Competence
values.definition: Being capable in the tasks I undertake

values.name: Diligence
values.definition: Applying sustained and careful effort

values.name: Precision
values.definition: Attending closely to correctness and detail

values.name: Reliability
values.definition: Doing what others can reasonably expect

values.name: Practicality
values.definition: Favoring workable responses to real constraints

values.name: Productivity
values.definition: Turning effort into valuable results

values.name: Innovation
values.definition: Putting novel ideas into effective practice

values.name: Leadership
values.definition: Helping a group choose direction and act well

values.name: Collaboration
values.definition: Working jointly toward shared outcomes

values.name: Quality
values.definition: Making things that hold up under serious use

values.name: Equality
values.definition: Supporting equal standing and opportunity

values.name: Liberty
values.definition: Protecting freedom from unjust control

values.name: Democracy
values.definition: Sharing public power through accountable participation

values.name: Tolerance
values.definition: Respecting people across meaningful difference

values.name: Pluralism
values.definition: Making room for multiple ways of living

values.name: Civic duty
values.definition: Contributing to functioning public life

values.name: Sustainability
values.definition: Meeting needs without undermining future life

values.name: Peacebuilding
values.definition: Transforming conflict without destructive force

values.name: Solidarity
values.definition: Standing with others around shared needs or injustice

values.name: Tradition
values.definition: Sustaining inherited practices that carry meaning

values.name: Security
values.definition: Maintaining dependable foundations and protection

values.name: Stability
values.definition: Preserving useful continuity over time

values.name: Order
values.definition: Keeping life and systems coherent and organized

values.name: Consistency
values.definition: Applying standards and effort predictably

values.name: Preparedness
values.definition: Anticipating needs and reducing preventable risk

values.name: Prosperity
values.definition: Having sufficient resources for a flourishing life

values.name: Wealth
values.definition: Accumulating substantial financial resources

values.name: Recognition
values.definition: Having meaningful work and qualities acknowledged

values.name: Influence
values.definition: Shaping decisions and conditions beyond myself

values.name: Authority
values.definition: Holding legitimate power and responsibility
